hero.badge: Founding Member Deal: First 10 makers get priority access + 1 month Pro
hero.subheadline: Noteship gives every update a public page, an in-app badge, and an email to every subscriber. Live in 5 minutes.
hero.urgency: ✦ {spotsLeft} founding spots left · first 10 get 1 month Pro free
hero.mockupEntry1Desc: Install our 'What's New' badge in your app. Users click to see all recent updates.
hero.mockupEntry2Desc: Your changelog lives at yourapp.com/changelog. Beautiful, brandable, fast.
hero.mockupEntry3Desc: Every update sent to subscribers automatically. Zero config required.
hero.mockupFix1Desc: Fixed an issue where the widget appeared behind modals in some apps.
hero.mockupFix2Desc: Resolved a UTF-8 issue causing garbled characters in notification emails.
hero.mockupFix3Desc: Auto dark/light mode now works correctly on Safari 17.

problem.title: You ship. They don't notice.
problem.subtitle: Most changelog tools are either abandoned, enterprise-priced, or a Notion doc nobody opens.
problem.card1Desc: Legacy tools charge $99/mo for a dying product. That's your entire MRR. And it still won't reach your users.
problem.card2Desc: A Notion doc won't engage anyone. Without a widget or notifications, your users never see it. Your updates deserve a stage.
problem.card3Title: Built for enterprise, not for makers
problem.card3Desc: Most changelog tools were designed for 50-person marketing teams. You need something that takes minutes, not a sprint.

solution.title: One tool. Three ways your users actually find out.
solution.feature1Desc: A dedicated page at yourapp.com/changelog. Chronological, filterable, fully branded. Not buried in docs. Not a Notion link. A real changelog page — the proof that you're still building.
solution.feature2Desc: A badge that lives inside your product. One click — users see every update. Install in 2 minutes. The features you build, finally seen.
solution.feature3Desc: Publish once. Every subscriber gets a clean email automatically. No sequences, no integrations, no extra work. Just reach.
solution.feature4Desc: Connect your repo and your changelog writes itself. Review the draft, hit publish. From merge to shipped story in seconds.

codeSnippet.title: Works with any stack.
codeSnippet.subtitle: One script tag. No npm install, no framework SDK, no config file.
codeSnippet.b1: Live on your domain in 2 minutes
codeSnippet.b3: Under 4 KB — zero impact on performance
codeSnippet.label: Add to your app

howItWorks.title: Live in 5 minutes. Seriously.
howItWorks.step1Desc: Sign up, name your project. Your public changelog URL is ready immediately — no setup, no config.
howItWorks.step2Desc: Write a clean entry in the editor. Add a tag, a version number, a screenshot. Hit publish.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Your page is live. Your widget updates. Every subscriber gets an email. Your users finally know.

testimonials.title: Early Access Reviews
testimonials.subtitle: Product launching this summer. Be one of the first 10 to shape what Noteship becomes.
testimonials.t1Text: I've been using a Notion doc as my changelog for 2 years. Nobody reads it. Noteship is exactly what I needed — a real page my users actually land on.
testimonials.t2Text: The in-app widget alone is worth it. I had users asking for features I'd already built weeks ago. That doesn't happen anymore.
testimonials.t3Text: I was paying $49/month for a changelog tool I hated. $3.99 and actually works for solo builders? Done.
testimonials.t4Text: The setup really is 5 minutes. I had my changelog live before my coffee got cold. The email notification to subscribers was the killer feature for me.
testimonials.t5Text: My users started commenting on updates they'd never have found otherwise. Retention went up. I attribute a chunk of that to Noteship.
testimonials.t6Text: Finally something built for indie makers, not enterprise teams. The pricing alone made me trust Martin's intent.
testimonials.b1Text: The branded changelog page looks more professional than my actual product homepage. My users trust the product more because they can see the momentum.
testimonials.b2Text: Before Noteship, I was manually emailing my subscribers about new features. Now it just happens. That alone saves me an hour a week.
testimonials.b3Text: The GitHub integration is what I'm most excited about. Writing changelogs from scratch was the thing I always skipped. Now it'll just happen.
testimonials.b4Text: I recommended Noteship to three other makers within the first week. It fills a gap that's been annoying me for years.
testimonials.b5Text: I love that it's one script tag. No npm, no config, no SDK. It just works. I wish every tool was this simple.
testimonials.b6Text: What sold me was reading Martin's founder story. You can tell this was built by someone who actually felt the pain. That's rare.

founderStory.title: Why I Built Noteship
founderStory.body: I'm a maker, just like you. I shipped features weekly... to crickets. My changelog was a dusty Notion doc that nobody read. I built Noteship to fix that — a simple, beautiful bridge between your hard work and your users. It's the tool I wish I'd had.
founderStory.philosophy: We handle the distribution. You write the story.

earlyAccess.title: Join the 10 makers who define the product
earlyAccess.subtitle: Launching this summer. 10 founding spots, first come first served. Not early users — the people who shape the roadmap.
earlyAccess.perk1: 1 month Pro free — no credit card, no conditions
earlyAccess.perk2: Direct line to the builder — not a support ticket
earlyAccess.perk3: Your feedback ships in days, not quarters
earlyAccess.perk4: Founding Member badge on your profile, forever

waitlistForm.success: You're in. Check your inbox for confirmation.
waitlistForm.errorInvalid: Please enter a valid email address.
waitlistForm.errorDuplicate: You're already on the list — we'll reach out soon!

faq.title: Questions.
faq.a1: This summer. Founding members get notified first and unlock 1 month Pro free with no strings attached.
faq.q2: Is there really a free plan?
faq.a2: Yes, forever. One project, up to 10 subscribers, and a full public changelog page — no credit card required. Enough to start and validate.
faq.q3: How does the widget embed work?
faq.a3: One script tag in your HTML. No npm install, no framework-specific SDK, no configuration file. It works with React, Vue, plain HTML — whatever you ship with.
faq.q4: What makes Noteship different from other changelog tools?
faq.a4: Most alternatives are either abandoned or priced for enterprise teams. Noteship is actively maintained, costs $3.99/month, and was built for indie makers — not companies with 50-person marketing teams.
faq.q5: What happens after I join the waitlist?
faq.a5: You get a confirmation email immediately. When we launch this summer, you'll be among the first to get access — with all your founding member perks intact.

pricing.title: Priced for makers, not enterprises.
pricing.badge: Less than a coffee per month
pricing.freeF2: Up to 10 subscribers — enough to start
pricing.proF7: White-Label: Put your brand front and center.
